subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Botulism and Canned Food
description: A 60-second lesson on Botulism and Canned Food.

hook.text: A single gram of botulinum toxin could kill a million people. It grows in improperly canned food.

buildup.text: Clostridium botulinum thrives in low-oxygen environments—like sealed jars and cans.

discovery.text: The toxin blocks nerve signals to muscles, causing progressive paralysis from head down.

twist.text: Bulging cans aren't just cosmetic defects—the gas inside is a sign of bacterial growth.

climax.text: Proper canning requires pressure cooking at 240°F to kill botulinum spores.

punchline.text: When the can bulges, the answer is always the same: throw it away.

quiz.question: Why does Clostridium botulinum thrive in canned food?
options: It feeds on metal from the can
